_meta.disclaimer: DEMONSTRATION — Simulated Inspection Data. This data is entirely fabricated for demonstration purposes. It does not represent any real vessel, facility, or inspection.

header.equipmentDescription: Vertical Column — Amine Absorber, Fabricated by Koch-Glitsch, 3200mm ID × 18400mm T/T, 42 valve trays
header.inspectorQualification: API 510 Certified, Certificate #68412, ASNT Level III UT

areasNotExamined: Mid-shell course trays 11–29 — client restricted access due to residual amine deposits; cleaned only at top and bottom sections
scopeAndLimitations.examinationLimitations: Vessel gas-freed and mechanically cleaned. Residual deposits on trays 11–29 limited internal visual in mid-section. UT readings taken externally at mid-shell TMLs to compensate.

methodology.utInstrument: Olympus 38DL PLUS, S/N UT-2024-1187, Cal due 2026-08-15
methodology.additionalNdtDetails: Dye penetrant testing applied to weld intersections at nozzles N3 and N5 per procedure NDT-WI-DPT-004 Rev.3. Sherwin D-70 developer system.
methodology.referenceProcedures: WI-UT-001 Rev.7, WI-VT-003 Rev.5, NDT-WI-DPT-004 Rev.3

visualFindings.location: Lower shell course, 260°–280° arc, 50–400mm above bottom tangent line
visualFindings.description: Broad area of general wall loss on internal surface over approximately 600mm × 350mm zone. Surface profile irregular with shallow undulations up to 1.2mm depth. Consistent with amine-side corrosion in the liquid sump region. No through-wall indications.
visualFindings.dimensions: 600mm × 350mm area, max depth 1.2mm from local surface
visualFindings.acceptanceCriteria: API 510 Section 7.4.2 — remaining thickness above minimum required per ASME VIII
visualFindings.recommendation: Continue monitoring at increased frequency. Add two additional TMLs (Shell-4-Low-A, Shell-4-Low-B) in the corroded zone. Evaluate amine chemistry and flow patterns to address root cause.

visualFindings.location: Nozzle N3 (amine inlet), internal weld toe at shell junction, 4–8 o'clock arc
visualFindings.description: Cluster of approximately 15 pits within 80mm × 40mm zone at the weld toe of nozzle N3 shell junction. Deepest pit measured at 2.8mm by pit gauge. Pitting follows the heat-affected zone. Consistent with amine stress corrosion cracking precursor pattern.
visualFindings.recommendation: FFS assessment required per API 579 Level 2 for pitting. DPT confirmed no surface-breaking cracks at present. Recommend TOFD at next examination to monitor for subsurface cracking initiation.

visualFindings.location: Bottom head, internal surface, centre of crown
visualFindings.description: Localised thinning consistent with impingement erosion from the bottom outlet nozzle N5 recirculation pattern. Smooth, polished surface appearance over approximately 200mm diameter area. UT readings (Head-B-N) confirm wall loss tracking with long-term trend.
visualFindings.dimensions: 200mm diameter area, gradual thinning — see TML Head-B-N trend
visualFindings.acceptanceCriteria: API 510 Section 7.4.2 — wall thickness above minimum required
visualFindings.recommendation: Continue monitoring at current TML locations. No operational change required at this time. Consider flow deflector at next turnaround if trend accelerates.

visualFindings.description: Three bolt holes on tray 10 downcomer flange show elongation of 2–3mm beyond original diameter. Bolt threads intact. Likely caused by thermal cycling induced movement. Tray remains structurally adequate but bolt pre-load may be reduced.
visualFindings.acceptanceCriteria: Manufacturer's installation specification — tray bolt pattern integrity
visualFindings.recommendation: Replace affected bolts with next size up (fitted bolts) at next turnaround. Monitor adjacent trays (9, 11) for similar pattern.

visualFindings.location: Nozzle N5 (bottom outlet), external flange face
visualFindings.description: Undercut observed on external fillet weld at nozzle N5 reinforcement pad, 9–11 o'clock arc (approximately 60mm length). Depth 1.5mm, within original fabrication acceptance criteria but at upper boundary. DPT performed — no crack indications. Likely original fabrication defect not previously recorded.
visualFindings.acceptanceCriteria: ASME VIII Div.1 UW-35 — undercut acceptance limits
visualFindings.recommendation: No remedial action required at this time. Record as baseline for future monitoring. DPT at next examination to confirm no crack initiation from the undercut root.

safetyDevices.notes: Set pressure confirmed at 13.75 bar (110% MAWP). Lift and reseat clean. Bench tested per API 576.
Redundant PRV. Set pressure confirmed. Full pop test satisfactory.
High level alarm function tested via simulation. Trips at 85% level as per P&ID.
High temperature alarm tested. Trips at 140°C (design temp +5°C margin).

fitnessForService.defectTypeAssessed: Localised pitting at Nozzle N3 (Finding F-002)
fitnessForService.conditionsForContinuedService: Vessel may continue in service at current MAWP provided: (1) TOFD examination of N3 weld HAZ within 30 days, (2) next internal examination within 3 years (not 5), (3) amine chemistry review to address root cause of pitting.

conclusions.conditionsForContinuedService: Subject to: (1) FFS conditions for N3 pitting — TOFD within 30 days, (2) enhanced monitoring of lower shell corrosion zone with additional TMLs, (3) next internal examination in 3 years maximum.
conclusions.summaryOfCriticalFindings: Five findings recorded. One requires engineering assessment (F-002: localised pitting at N3 nozzle weld HAZ — FFS assessment completed, fit with conditions). One requires action before next examination (F-001: general corrosion in lower shell sump zone — within limits but accelerating). Three acceptable for monitoring. Overall wall loss rates within expected range for amine service. Bottom head and nozzle N5 showing highest long-term rates — within acceptable limits but warrant continued attention.
conclusions.recommendedIntervalJustification: 3-year interval selected (reduced from standard 5-year) based on: (1) FFS conditions requiring enhanced monitoring, (2) accelerating short-term corrosion rate at Shell-4 zone (0.47 mm/yr short-term vs 0.26 mm/yr long-term), (3) remaining life at thinnest point (Shell-4-Low) is 6.8 years — 3-year interval provides adequate safety margin per API 510 Section 6.4.
